title: Tamil Nadu sends on May 19, the first consignment of rice, milk powder and essential medicines Via Ship  to Sri Lanka signalling a shift in  relations between people of the  South Indian State nd the Island
body_lines: When Tan Binh 99, the Panama-registered general cargo vessel left Chennai at 8.28 a.m. on May 19 with 9,000 tons of rice, 200 tons of milk powder, and 24 tons of essential medicines, it marked a first of sorts: A province in a developing country extending its assistance to another country.

Tamil Nadu decided to extend humanitarian assistance to Sri Lanka in the form of rice and other essential commodities, taking into consideration the plight of the people following the economic deterioration of the island nation. This is a marked shift in the stand of political parties in Tamil Nadu, which has in the past demanded a separate Tamil nation in Sri Lanka. It also sets the stage for enhanced interaction between the people of the two regions.

Chief Minister M.K. Stalin flagged off the process of loading the cargo onto the vessel on May 18. At a function held at West Quay in Chennai Port on May 18, Stalin symbolically handed over a sample pouch of the material to D. Venkateshwaran, Sri Lankan Deputy High Commissioner in Southern India.. The cargo vessel is expected to cover the distance of just over 400 nautical miles in about two days.

The total aid promised by the Tamil Nadu government is worth Rs.123 crore and consists of 40,000 tons of rice, 100-plus life-saving drugs and 500 tons of milk powder. This is just the first consignment and is being sent with the consent of the Government of India. The State government has appealed to the people to contribute generously to the relief efforts in Sri Lanka.

In a Legislative Assembly resolution, all the political parties represented in the Tamil Nadu State Assembly had expressed their anxiety over the rising prices of essential commodities in Sri Lanka and the implications for India if the situation did not improve.

Tamil Nadu had extensive trade, cultural, and cricketing ties with Sri Lanka, but the relationship came to a standstill after the crushing defeat of the the Liberation Tamils of Tamil Eelam (LTTE) in May 2009. In fact, Tamil nationalist groups and fringe elements in the State had agitated against the training given to Sri Lankan officers in defence facilities located in the State and had protested the presence of Sri Lankan cricketers in the Chennai franchise of the Indian Premier League. On its part, the Sri Lankan government stipulated that no Sri Lankan government official could enter Tamil Nadu or transit via the Chennai airport given the security situation.

Tamil Nadu took the first step on March 31, when Chief Minister M.K. Stalin raised the issue of helping Sri Lankan Tamils with Prime Minister Narendra Modi as a trickle of refugees began arriving in the State from Sri Lanka when the economic situation in Sri Lanka worsened. The first set of refugees—technically economic refugees since there was no war in Sri Lanka—arrived in the State on March 8. By April 25, the number of refugees swelled to 75. “Many more would have crossed over but for the Sri Lankan Navy arresting or sending back Sri Lankan Tamils,” a senior Tamil Nadu officer told Frontline.

Tamil Nadu’s offer was welcomed by the Tamil political parties in Sri Lanka. The Tamil National Alliance (TNA), the largest umbrella alliance of Tamil political parties in Sri Lanka, welcomed the move. But, after a few days, given the new-found bonhomie between all sections of the people in Sri Lanka, the TNA requested the Tamil Nadu government to include all Sri Lankans in the proposed material aid package.

There was a lull soon after, with the Indian government not taking a decision on Tamil Nadu’s request. On April 15, Stalin wrote to the External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar, reiterating the Tamil Nadu government’s commitment to “ship essential supplies including food grain, vegetables, and medicines from Thoothukudi Port to the Sri Lankan Tamils living in the northern and eastern parts of Sri Lanka, as well as those working in the plantations”. He had earlier raised the issue in a telephone conversation with him on April 7.

Making a statement in the Assembly ahead of a resolution helping all Sri Lankans, Stalin said that he did not want to dwell into how Sri Lanka ended up in a situation of this nature. At the same time, the hardship faced by the people of Sri Lanka had saddened everyone in Tamil Nadu. Even life-saving medicines were hard to find. The price of all essential items had gone up beyond reach of the common man. “We cannot merely carry on with our lives thinking that this is the problem of a neighbouring country. This is not the time to be drawn into details of who is in power and what kind of people they are,” he said in a reference to the fact that President Gotabaya Rajapaksa was the Defence Secretary and the current Prime Minister, Mahinda Rajapaksa was the President when LTTE chief V. Prabhakaran was killed and thousands of Tamils were murdered as the Eelam war came to a close.

“We have to help in whatever way we can. As soon as we began getting news of the problems there, I had announced that we will help Eelam Tamils in all the ways that we can…. Some Tamil organisations in Sri Lanka requested me not to send relief only for Tamils, but to send it to all Sri Lankans. ‘Please do not view people by their ethnic identities. All of us are facing the same problems,’ they informed us. When I heard this, I become emotional…. This is Tamil culture. This goes to show that Sri Lankan Tamils are living to the maxim ‘the good heart will bless even the enemy.’” (This is a line penned by poet Subramaniya Bharati—pagaivanukkum arulvai nannenje.)

On April 29, the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly passed a revised resolution, altering its earlier stance and saying that it was willing to provide humanitarian assistance to all Sri Lankan people. The same day, in a letter addressed to Narendra Modi, Stalin said: “Reports of untold sufferings and unrest in our neighbourhood has been pouring in every day. Considering this critical situation, the august House of the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly has unanimously passed a resolution today, urging the Government of India to positively consider our request to immediately despatch the much needed supplies.”

TNA leader M.A. Sumanthiran welcomed the statement and conveyed his “heartfelt thanks to the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister for heeding to our request and providing assistance to all Sri Lankans”. Mano Ganesan, Tamil Progressive Alliance leader and Colombo MP, tweeted on April 29: “Thank you CM Stalin of TN for the bighearted decision to send a shipload donation of food and medicine to the value of nearly LKR 6000 million to all the people of Sri Lanka. We now foresee the consents of the Government of India to this aid mission of Government of Tamil Nadu.”

After refusing to respond Stalin’s March 31 demand, the Union government finally agreed to allow the request of the Tamil Nadu government. On May 1, Jaishankar wrote to Stalin saying that Sri Lanka was willing to accept “inclusive aid on a Government-to-Government basis”. “The matter could, therefore, be coordinated by our High Commission, supplementing assistance already underway,” he said and added that the Tamil Nadu Chief Secretary could coordinate with Government of India for the supply and distribution of humanitarian relief.

On May 4, the then Sri Lankan Prime Minister Mahinda Rajapaksa wrote to Stalin, thanking him for the initiative: “I wish to thank you and the Tamil Nadu government on behalf of the people of Sri Lanka, for viewing the country’s crisis from a humanitarian standpoint, rather than as a problem concerning another country.”

While Tamil Nadu’s actions stem from the fact that the problem in Sri Lanka affects the Tamil-speaking people, the Government of India views it from the angle of controlling refugee inflows and improving relations between India and Sri Lanka using Tamil Nadu.

The trade and other connections between the people of the two countries, predominantly between Colombo and Chennai, had wound down after 2009. During the war years and earlier, the two countries were connected by two ferry services, one between Rameswaram (the southern-most point of Indian mainland) and Mannar (the nearest point in Sri Lanka), and the other between Thoothukudi and Colombo.

Though the Thoothukudi-Colombo service was revived in 2011, the service had to be withdrawn for lack of patronage. With the possible increase in people-to-people ties, New Delhi hopes to revive the traditional trade routes as well as the traditional links between the Tamil-speaking people of both countries.

But all these hinge on the kind of economic recovery in Sri Lanka. The Sri Lankan Central Bank said on May 19 that economic activity is expected to be “affected considerably” because of the fuel shortage and supply issues. Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe announced in Parliament that only essential service staff had to report for work on May 20. All other employees have been asked to stay away because of the fuel crisis.
The Prime Minister also informed Parliament that petrol and diesel required up to mid-June has already been procured. It appears that there will be as many ups as downs in the road ahead.

si.title: දකුණු ඉන්දීය ප්‍රාන්තයේ සහ දිවයිනේ ජනතාව අතර සබඳතාවල වෙනසක් සනිටුහන් කරමින් තමිල්නාඩු ප්‍රථම සහල්, කිරිපිටි සහ අත්‍යාවශ්‍ය ඖෂධ තොගය ශ්‍රී ලංකාවට නෞකාවක් හරහා මැයි 19 වැනිදා එවයි.
body_lines: Tan Binh 99, පැනමාවේ ලියාපදිංචි සාමාන්‍ය භාණ්ඩ ප්‍රවාහන නෞකාව සහල් ටොන් 9,000 ක්, කිරිපිටි ටොන් 200 ක් සහ අත්‍යාවශ්‍ය ඖෂධ ටොන් 24 ක් සමඟ මැයි 19 වැනි දින පෙ.ව. 8.28 ට චෙන්නායි නුවරින් පිටත් වූ විට, එය පළමු වර්ගයේ ප්‍රාන්තයක් ලෙස සටහන් විය. සංවර්ධනය වෙමින් පවතින රටක් වෙනත් රටකට තම ආධාර ලබා දීම.

තමිල්නාඩුව ශ්‍රී ලංකාවට සහල් සහ අනෙකුත් අත්‍යවශ්‍ය භාණ්ඩ වශයෙන් මානුෂීය ආධාර ලබා දීමට තීරණය කළේ දිවයිනේ ජාතියේ ආර්ථික පරිහානියත් සමඟ ජනතාව මුහුණ දෙන දුෂ්කරතා සැලකිල්ලට ගනිමින්. මෙය අතීතයේ ශ්‍රී ලංකාව තුළ වෙනම දෙමළ ජාතියක් ඉල්ලා සිටි තමිල්නාඩුවේ දේශපාලන පක්ෂවල ස්ථාවරයේ කැපී පෙනෙන වෙනසක් වේ. එය කලාප දෙකේ ජනතාව අතර වැඩිදියුණු වූ අන්තර්ක්‍රියා සඳහා වේදිකාව ද සකසයි.

මහ ඇමැති එම්.කේ. ස්ටාලින් විසින් මැයි 18 වන දින යාත්‍රාවට භාණ්ඩ පැටවීමේ ක්‍රියාවලිය ආරම්භ කරන ලදී. මැයි 18 වන දින චෙන්නායි වරායේ වෙස්ට් ක්වේ හි පැවති උත්සවයකදී, ස්ටාලින් විසින් එම ද්‍රව්‍යයේ සාම්පල මල්ලක් සංකේතාත්මකව ශ්‍රී ලංකා නියෝජ්‍ය මහ කොමසාරිස් ඩී. වෙන්කේෂ්වරන් වෙත භාර දෙන ලදී. දකුණු ඉන්දියාවේ.. භාණ්ඩ ප්‍රවාහන නෞකාව දින දෙකකින් පමණ නාවික සැතපුම් 400කට වැඩි දුරක් ආවරණය කිරීමට බලාපොරොත්තු වේ.

තමිල්නාඩු රජය විසින් පොරොන්දු වූ මුළු ආධාරය රුපියල් කෝටි 123 ක් වන අතර සහල් ටොන් 40,000 ක්, ජීවිතාරක්ෂක ඖෂධ 100 කට වැඩි ප්‍රමාණයක් සහ කිරිපිටි ටොන් 500 කින් සමන්විත වේ. මෙය පළමු භාණ්ඩ තොගය වන අතර ඉන්දීය රජයේ අනුමැතිය ඇතිව යවනු ලැබේ. ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ සහන කටයුතු සඳහා නොමසුරුව දායක වන ලෙස ප්‍රාන්ත රජය ජනතාවගෙන් ඉල්ලා සිටී.

ව්‍යවස්ථාදායක සභා යෝජනාවකින් තමිල්නාඩු ප්‍රාන්ත සභාව නියෝජනය කරන සියලුම දේශපාලන පක්ෂ ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ අත්‍යවශ්‍ය භාණ්ඩවල මිල ඉහළ යාම සහ තත්ත්වය යහපත් නොවන්නේ නම් ඉන්දියාවට ඇති විය හැකි බලපෑම් පිළිබඳව කනස්සල්ල පළ කර තිබුණි.

තමිල්නාඩුවට ශ්‍රී ලංකාව සමඟ පුළුල් වෙළෙඳ, සංස්කෘතික සහ ක්‍රිකට් සබඳතා පැවති නමුත්, 2009 මැයි මාසයේදී දෙමළ ඊළාම් විමුක්ති දෙමළ (එල්ටීටීඊ) ත්‍රස්තවාදී පරාජයෙන් පසු එම සබඳතාව නතර විය. ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම, දෙමළ ජාතිකවාදී කණ්ඩායම් සහ අන්තවාදී කොටස් ප්‍රාන්තය තුළ ශ්‍රී ලංකා නිලධාරීන්ට ප්‍රාන්තයේ පිහිටි ආරක්ෂක පහසුකම් පිළිබඳ පුහුණුව ලබා දීමට එරෙහිව උද්ඝෝෂණ කර ඇති අතර ඉන්දීය ප්‍රිමියර් ලීග් තරඟාවලියේ චෙන්නායි තරඟාවලියේ ශ්‍රී ලංකා ක්‍රිකට් ක්‍රීඩකයින් පැමිණීමට විරෝධය පළ කර තිබුණි. එහි පැත්තෙන්, ශ්‍රී ලංකා රජය කිසිදු ශ්‍රී ලංකා රජයේ නිලධාරියෙකුට තමිල්නාඩුවට ඇතුළු වීමට හෝ චෙන්නායි ගුවන් තොටුපළ හරහා සංක්‍රමණය වීමට නොහැකි බව නියම කළේ ආරක්ෂක තත්ත්වය මත ය.

තමිල්නාඩුව පළමු පියවර තැබුවේ මාර්තු 31 වැනිදා මහ ඇමැති එම්.කේ. ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ආර්ථික තත්ත්වය නරක අතට හැරෙන විට ශ්‍රී ලංකාවෙන් සරණාගතයින් රැල්ලක් ප්‍රාන්තයට පැමිණීමට පටන් ගත් විට ස්ටාලින් ශ්‍රී ලාංකික දෙමළ ජනතාවට උදව් කිරීමේ ප්‍රශ්නය අගමැති නරේන්ද්‍ර මෝදි සමඟ මතු කළේය. ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ යුද්ධයක් නොතිබූ පසු පළමු සරණාගතයින් කණ්ඩායම - තාක්ෂණික වශයෙන් ආර්ථික සරණාගතයින් - මාර්තු 8 වන දින ප්‍රාන්තයට පැමිණියහ. අප්‍රේල් 25 වන විට සරණාගතයින් සංඛ්‍යාව 75 දක්වා ඉහළ ගියේය. නාවික හමුදාව ශ්‍රී ලාංකික ද්‍රවිඩයන් අත්අඩංගුවට ගැනීම හෝ ආපසු එවීම,” තමිල්නාඩු ජ්‍යෙෂ්ඨ නිලධාරියෙක් ෆ්‍රන්ට්ලයින් වෙත පැවසීය.

තමිල්නාඩුවේ මෙම යෝජනාව ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ දෙමළ දේශපාලන පක්ෂ විසින් පිළිගනු ලැබීය. ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ දෙමළ දේශපාලන පක්ෂවල විශාලතම කුඩ සන්ධානය වන දෙමළ ජාතික සන්ධානය (ටීඑන්ඒ) මෙම පියවර පිළිගත්තේය. එහෙත්, දින කිහිපයකට පසු, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ සියලු ජන කොටස් අතර අලුතින් ඇති වූ ප්‍රසාදය සැලකිල්ලට ගනිමින්, යෝජිත ද්‍රව්‍ය ආධාර පැකේජයට සියලුම ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන් ඇතුළත් කරන ලෙස TNA තමිල්නාඩු රජයෙන් ඉල්ලා සිටියේය.

තමිල්නාඩුවේ ඉල්ලීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් ඉන්දීය රජය තීරණයක් නොගැනීමත් සමඟ වැඩි කල් නොගොස් නිස්කලංක තත්ත්වයක් ඇති විය. අප්‍රේල් 15දා, ස්ටාලින් විදේශ කටයුතු ඇමති එස්. ජයශංකර් වෙත ලිපියක් යවමින්, “උතුරු සහ නැගෙනහිර ප්‍රදේශවල වෙසෙන ශ්‍රී ලාංකික දෙමළ ජනයාට තූත්තුකුඩි වරායෙන් ආහාර ධාන්‍ය, එළවළු සහ ඖෂධ ඇතුළු අත්‍යාවශ්‍ය සැපයුම් නැව්ගත කිරීමට තමිල්නාඩු ආන්ඩුව කැපවීම යලි අවධාරණය කළේය. ශ්‍රී ලංකාව මෙන්ම වතුකරයේ වැඩ කරන අයද. ඔහු මීට පෙර අප්‍රේල් 7 වැනිදා ඔහු සමඟ දුරකථන සංවාදයකදී මෙම ප්‍රශ්නය මතු කර ඇත.

සියලුම ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන්ට උපකාර කිරීමේ යෝජනාවකට පෙර සභාවේදී ප්‍රකාශයක් කරමින් ස්ටාලින් කියා සිටියේ ශ්‍රී ලංකාව මෙවන් තත්ත්වයකට පත් වූයේ කෙසේද යන්න පිළිබඳව තවදුරටත් කතා කිරීමට තමාට අවශ්‍ය නොවන බවයි. ඒ සමගම ශ්‍රී ලංකාවාසී ජනතාව මුහුණ දුන් දුෂ්කරතා තමිල්නාඩුවේ සියලු දෙනාගේම කනගාටුවට හේතු වී තිබුණි. ජීවිතාරක්ෂක ඖෂධ පවා සොයා ගැනීමට අපහසු විය. සියලුම අත්‍යවශ්‍ය ද්‍රව්‍යවල මිල සාමාන්‍ය ජනතාවට දරාගත නොහැකි ලෙස ඉහළ ගොස් තිබිණි. “මෙය අසල්වැසි රටක ප්‍රශ්නයක් යැයි සිතා අපට නිකම්ම ජීවිතය ගෙන යා නොහැක. බලයේ සිටින්නේ කවුරුන්ද, ඔවුන් කුමන ආකාරයේ පුද්ගලයන්ද යන්න ගැන විස්තර කිරීමට මෙය කාලය නොවේ," ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ මහතා ආරක්ෂක ලේකම්වරයා වූ අතර වත්මන් අගමැති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ මහතා බව සඳහන් කරමින් ඔහු පැවසීය. ඊලාම් යුද්ධය අවසන් වීමත් සමඟ කොටි නායක වී. ප්‍රභාකරන් ඝාතනය කර දහස් ගණන් දෙමළ ජනතාව ඝාතනය කළ අවස්ථාවේදී ජනාධිපතිවරයා.

“අපිට පුළුවන් විදිහට උදව් කරන්න ඕන. අපට එහි ඇති ගැටලු පිළිබඳ ආරංචිය ලැබීමට පටන් ගත් වහාම, අපි අපට හැකි සෑම ආකාරයකින්ම ඊළාම් දෙමළ ජනයාට උපකාර කරන බව මම ප්‍රකාශ කළෙමි. ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ඇතැම් දෙමළ සංවිධාන මගෙන් ඉල්ලීම් කළේ දෙමළ ජනතාවට පමණක් සහන එවන්න එපා, සියලුම ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන්ට යවන ලෙසයි. ‘කරුණාකර මිනිසුන් ඔවුන්ගේ වාර්ගික අනන්‍යතාවයෙන් නොබලන්න. අපි හැමෝම එකම ප්‍රශ්නවලට මුහුණ දෙනවා.’ ඔවුන් අපට දැනුම් දුන්නා. මේක ඇහුවම හිතට දැනුනේ හැගීමක්.... මේක දෙමළ සංස්කෘතිය. ශ්‍රී ලාංකේය දෙමළ ජනතාව ‘යහපත් සිත සතුරාට පවා ආශීර්වාද කරයි’ යන උපරිමයෙන් ජීවත් වන බව මෙයින් පෙන්නුම් කෙරේ.” (මෙය සුබ්‍රමනිය භාරතී කවියාගේ පද පේලියකි—පගයිවනුක්කුම් අරුල්වයි නන්නෙන්ජේ.)

අප්‍රේල් 29 දා තමිල්නාඩු ව්‍යවස්ථාදායක සභාව සිය පෙර ස්ථාවරය වෙනස් කරමින් සියලුම ශ්‍රී ලාංකික ජනතාවට මානුෂීය ආධාර ලබාදීමට කැමති බව පවසමින් සංශෝධිත යෝජනාවක් සම්මත කළේය. එදිනම, නරේන්ද්‍ර මෝඩි වෙත ලිපියක් යවමින් ස්ටාලින් මෙසේ පැවසීය: “අපගේ අසල්වැසි ප්‍රදේශයේ කිව නොහැකි දුක් වේදනා සහ නොසන්සුන්තාව පිළිබඳ වාර්තා සෑම දිනකම ගලා එයි. මෙම තීරණාත්මක තත්ත්වය සැලකිල්ලට ගනිමින්, තමිල්නාඩු ව්‍යවස්ථාදායක සභාවේ උත්තරීතර සභාව අද ඒකමතිකව යෝජනාවක් සම්මත කර ඇති අතර, අත්‍යවශ්‍ය සැපයුම් වහාම යැවීමට අපගේ ඉල්ලීම ධනාත්මකව සලකා බලන ලෙස ඉන්දීය රජයෙන් ඉල්ලා සිටී.

TNA නායක M.A. සුමන්තිරන් එම ප්‍රකාශය පිළිගත් අතර “අපගේ ඉල්ලීමට අවනත වී සියලුම ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයින්ට සහය ලබා දීම සම්බන්ධයෙන් තමිල්නාඩු ප්‍රධාන අමාත්‍යවරයාට හෘදයාංගම ස්තුතිය” ප්‍රකාශ කළේය. දෙමළ ප්‍රගතිශීලී සන්ධානයේ නායක සහ කොළඹ පාර්ලිමේන්තු මන්ත්‍රී මනෝ ගනේෂන් අප්‍රේල් 29 දා ට්වීට් කළේය: “ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ සියලුම ජනතාවට රුපියල් මිලියන 6000 කට ආසන්න වටිනාකමකින් යුත් ආහාර සහ ඖෂධ නැව් ප්‍රමාණයක් පරිත්‍යාග කිරීමට ගත් දැඩි තීරණය ගැන TN හි CM Stalin ඔබට ස්තූතියි. ලංකා. තමිල්නාඩු රජයේ මෙම ආධාර මෙහෙයුමට ඉන්දීය රජයේ කැමැත්ත අපි දැන් අපේක්ෂා කරමු.”

ස්ටාලින්ගේ මාර්තු 31 ඉල්ලීමට ප්‍රතිචාර දැක්වීම ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කිරීමෙන් පසුව, තමිල්නාඩු ආන්ඩුවේ ඉල්ලීමට ඉඩ දීමට යුනියන් ආන්ඩුව අවසානයේ එකඟ විය. මැයි 1 දා, ජයිශංකර් ස්ටාලින්ට ලිවීය, ශ්‍රී ලංකාව "ආණ්ඩුවෙන් රජයට පදනම් වූ ආධාර" පිළිගැනීමට කැමති බව පවසමින්. "එබැවින්, අපගේ මහ කොමසාරිස්වරයා විසින් මෙම කාරණය සම්බන්ධීකරණය කළ හැකි අතර, දැනටමත් ක්‍රියාත්මක වන ආධාර අතිරේකව," මානුෂීය සහන සැපයීම සහ බෙදා හැරීම සඳහා තමිල්නාඩු ප්‍රධාන ලේකම්වරයාට ඉන්දීය රජය සමඟ සම්බන්ධීකරණය කළ හැකි බව ඔහු පැවසීය.

මැයි 4 දා, එවකට ශ්‍රී ලංකා අගමැති මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ ස්ටාලින්ට ලිපියක් යවමින්, මුලපිරීම සඳහා ඔහුට ස්තූති කරමින්: “රටේ අර්බුදය මානුෂීයව සිට බැලීම ගැන ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ජනතාව වෙනුවෙන් මම ඔබට සහ තමිල්නාඩු රජයට ස්තූතිවන්ත වෙමි. වෙනත් රටක ගැටලුවක් ලෙස නොව ස්ථාවරය.

තමිල්නාඩුවේ ක්‍රියාවන් පැන නගින්නේ ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ගැටලුව දෙමළ කතා කරන ජනතාවට බලපාන නිසා වන අතර, ඉන්දීය රජය එය දෙස බලන්නේ සරණාගතයින් ගලා ඒම පාලනය කිරීම සහ තමිල්නාඩුව භාවිතා කරමින් ඉන්දියාව සහ ශ්‍රී ලංකාව අතර සබඳතා වැඩිදියුණු කිරීමේ කෝණයෙනි.

ප්‍රධාන වශයෙන් කොළඹ සහ චෙන්නායි අතර දෙරටේ ජනතාව අතර වෙළඳාම සහ අනෙකුත් සම්බන්ධතා 2009 න් පසු බිඳ වැටුණි. යුධ සමයේ සහ ඊට පෙර, දෙරට යාත්‍රා සේවා දෙකකින් සම්බන්ධ කරන ලදී, එකක් රාමේෂ්වරම් (දකුණු කෙළවරේ) ඉන්දියානු ප්‍රධාන භූමියේ ලක්ෂ්‍යය) සහ මන්නාරම (ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ආසන්නතම ස්ථානය) සහ අනෙක තූතුකුඩි සහ කොළඹ අතර වේ.

2011 වසරේ තූතුකුඩි-කොළඹ සේවාව යළි පණ ගැන්වුණ ද අනුග්‍රහය නොලැබීම හේතුවෙන් එම සේවාව ඉවත් කර ගැනීමට සිදු විය. මිනිසුන් අතර සබඳතා වර්ධනය වීමත් සමඟ දෙරටේ දෙමළ කතා කරන ජනතාව අතර සාම්ප්‍රදායික වෙළෙඳ මාර්ග මෙන්ම සම්ප්‍රදායික සම්බන්ධතා ද පුනර්ජීවනය කිරීමට නවදිල්ලිය බලාපොරොත්තු වේ.

නමුත් මේ සියල්ල ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ආර්ථික ප්‍රකෘතිය මත රඳා පවතී. ඉන්ධන හිඟය සහ සැපයුම් ගැටළු හේතුවෙන් ආර්ථික ක්‍රියාකාරකම් "සැලකිය යුතු ලෙස බලපානු ඇතැයි" අපේක්ෂා කරන බව ශ්‍රී ලංකා මහ බැංකුව මැයි 19 දා ප්‍රකාශ කළේය. මැයි 20 වැනිදා සේවයට වාර්තා කළ යුත්තේ අත්‍යාවශ්‍ය සේවා කාර්ය මණ්ඩලයට පමණක් බව අගමැති රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ මහතා පාර්ලිමේන්තුවේදී ප්‍රකාශ කළේය. ඉන්ධන අර්බුදය හේතුවෙන් අනෙකුත් සියලුම සේවකයන්ට ඉවත්ව සිටින ලෙස දැනුම් දී තිබේ.
ජූනි මස මැද දක්වා අවශ්‍ය පෙට්‍රල් සහ ඩීසල් දැනටමත් ලබාගෙන අවසන් බවද අගමැතිවරයා පාර්ලිමේන්තුවට දැනුම් දුන්නේය. ඉදිරි මාවතේ උඩු යටිකුරු වන තරම් පහත වැටීම් ඇති බව පෙනේ.

ta.title: தென்னிந்திய மாநிலம் மற்றும் தீவு மக்களுக்கு இடையிலான உறவில் மாற்றம் ஏற்பட்டுள்ளதைக் குறிக்கும் வகையில், அரிசி, பால் பவுடர் மற்றும் அத்தியாவசிய மருந்துகளின் முதல் சரக்குகளை மே 19 அன்று தமிழ்நாடு கப்பல் மூலம் இலங்கைக்கு அனுப்புகிறது.
body_lines: Tan Binh 99, பனாமாவில் பதிவுசெய்யப்பட்ட பொது சரக்குக் கப்பல் 9,000 டன் அரிசி, 200 டன் பால் பவுடர் மற்றும் 24 டன் அத்தியாவசிய மருந்துகளுடன் மே 19 அன்று காலை 8.28 மணிக்கு சென்னையில் இருந்து புறப்பட்டபோது, ​​அது முதல் வகையாகக் குறித்தது: ஒரு மாகாணத்தில் ஒரு மாகாணம் வளரும் நாடு மற்றொரு நாட்டிற்கு உதவி செய்கிறது.

தீவு தேசத்தின் பொருளாதார சீரழிவைத் தொடர்ந்து மக்கள் படும் துயரங்களைக் கருத்தில் கொண்டு, அரிசி மற்றும் பிற அத்தியாவசியப் பொருட்களின் வடிவில் மனிதாபிமான உதவிகளை இலங்கைக்கு வழங்க தமிழ்நாடு முடிவு செய்தது. கடந்த காலங்களில் இலங்கையில் தனித் தமிழ் நாடு கோரிய தமிழ்நாட்டின் அரசியல் கட்சிகளின் நிலைப்பாட்டில் இது ஒரு குறிப்பிடத்தக்க மாற்றமாகும். இது இரு பிராந்திய மக்களிடையே மேம்பட்ட தொடர்புக்கு களம் அமைக்கிறது.

முதல்வர் மு.க. மே 18 அன்று கப்பலில் சரக்குகளை ஏற்றும் பணியை ஸ்டாலின் கொடியசைத்து தொடங்கி வைத்தார். மே 18 அன்று சென்னை துறைமுகத்தில் உள்ள வெஸ்ட் க்வேயில் நடைபெற்ற நிகழ்ச்சியில், இலங்கை துணை உயர் ஆணையர் டி. வெங்கடேஷ்வரனிடம் ஸ்டாலின் அடையாளப் பொருளின் மாதிரி பையை வழங்கினார். தென்னிந்தியாவில்.. சரக்குக் கப்பல் சுமார் 400 நாட்டிகல் மைல் தூரத்தை சுமார் இரண்டு நாட்களில் கடக்கும் என்று எதிர்பார்க்கப்படுகிறது.

தமிழக அரசு உறுதியளித்த மொத்த உதவித் தொகை ரூ.123 கோடியாகும், இதில் 40,000 டன் அரிசி, 100-க்கும் மேற்பட்ட உயிர்காக்கும் மருந்துகள் மற்றும் 500 டன் பால் பவுடர் ஆகியவை அடங்கும். இது முதல் சரக்கு மற்றும் இந்திய அரசின் ஒப்புதலுடன் அனுப்பப்படுகிறது. இலங்கையில் நிவாரணப் பணிகளுக்கு தாராளமாக பங்களிக்குமாறு மாநில அரசு மக்களை கேட்டுக் கொண்டுள்ளது.

ஒரு சட்டப் பேரவைத் தீர்மானத்தில், தமிழ்நாடு மாநிலப் பேரவையில் அங்கம் வகிக்கும் அனைத்து அரசியல் கட்சிகளும் இலங்கையில் அத்தியாவசியப் பொருட்களின் விலையேற்றம் குறித்தும், நிலைமை சீரடையாவிட்டால் இந்தியாவுக்கு ஏற்படும் பாதிப்புகள் குறித்தும் கவலை தெரிவித்துள்ளன.

தமிழ்நாடு இலங்கையுடன் விரிவான வர்த்தக, கலாச்சார மற்றும் கிரிக்கெட் உறவுகளைக் கொண்டிருந்தது, ஆனால் மே 2009 இல் தமிழீழ விடுதலைப் புலிகளின் (LTTE) நசுக்கிய தோல்விக்குப் பின்னர் அந்த உறவு ஸ்தம்பிதமடைந்தது. உண்மையில், தமிழ் தேசியவாத குழுக்கள் மற்றும் விளிம்புநிலை கூறுகள் மாநிலத்தில் அமைந்துள்ள பாதுகாப்பு வசதிகளில் இலங்கை அதிகாரிகளுக்கு வழங்கப்படும் பயிற்சிக்கு எதிராக மாநிலத்தில் கிளர்ந்தெழுந்தது மற்றும் இந்தியன் பிரீமியர் லீக்கின் சென்னை உரிமையில் இலங்கை கிரிக்கெட் வீரர்கள் இருப்பதற்கு எதிர்ப்புத் தெரிவித்திருந்தது. பாதுகாப்புச் சூழலைக் கருத்தில் கொண்டு, இலங்கை அரசு அதிகாரிகள் யாரும் தமிழகத்திற்குள் நுழையவோ அல்லது சென்னை விமான நிலையம் வழியாகச் செல்லவோ முடியாது என்று இலங்கை அரசு தனது தரப்பில் நிபந்தனை விதித்தது.

தமிழகம் முதல் அடியை மார்ச் 31-ம் தேதி எடுத்து, முதல்வர் மு.க. இலங்கையில் பொருளாதார நிலை மோசமடைந்தபோது இலங்கையிலிருந்து அகதிகள் துள்ளிக்குதிக்கத் தொடங்கியதால், பிரதமர் நரேந்திர மோடியிடம் இலங்கைத் தமிழர்களுக்கு உதவுவது குறித்து ஸ்டாலின் கேள்வி எழுப்பினார். முதல் அகதிகள்-இலங்கையில் போர் இல்லாததால் தொழில்நுட்ப ரீதியாக பொருளாதார அகதிகள்- மார்ச் 8 ஆம் தேதி மாநிலத்தை வந்தடைந்தனர். ஏப்ரல் 25 க்குள் அகதிகளின் எண்ணிக்கை 75 ஆக உயர்ந்தது. "இன்னும் பலர் கடந்து சென்றிருப்பார்கள் ஆனால் இலங்கைக்கு இலங்கைத் தமிழர்களை கடற்படை கைது செய்கிறது அல்லது திருப்பி அனுப்புகிறது” என்று தமிழக மூத்த அதிகாரி ஒருவர் ஃப்ரண்ட்லைனிடம் தெரிவித்தார்.

தமிழகத்தின் இந்த சலுகையை இலங்கையில் உள்ள தமிழ் அரசியல் கட்சிகள் வரவேற்றுள்ளன. இலங்கையில் உள்ள தமிழ் அரசியல் கட்சிகளின் மிகப்பெரிய குடை கூட்டணியான தமிழ் தேசிய கூட்டமைப்பு (TNA) இந்த நடவடிக்கையை வரவேற்றுள்ளது. ஆனால், சில நாட்களுக்குப் பிறகு, இலங்கையில் அனைத்துத் தரப்பு மக்களுக்கும் இடையே புதிதாகக் காணப்பட்ட பொன்போமியைக் கருத்தில் கொண்டு, உத்தேச பொருள் உதவிப் பொதியில் அனைத்து இலங்கையர்களையும் சேர்த்துக்கொள்ளுமாறு தமிழ்த் தேசியக் கூட்டமைப்பு தமிழக அரசிடம் கோரிக்கை விடுத்தது.

தமிழகத்தின் கோரிக்கை மீது இந்திய அரசு எந்த முடிவும் எடுக்காததால் சிறிது நேரத்திலேயே அமைதி ஏற்பட்டது. ஏப்ரல் 15ஆம் தேதி, வெளியுறவுத்துறை அமைச்சர் எஸ். ஜெய்சங்கருக்கு ஸ்டாலின் கடிதம் எழுதி, “உணவு தானியங்கள், காய்கறிகள், மருந்துகள் உள்ளிட்ட அத்தியாவசியப் பொருட்களை தூத்துக்குடி துறைமுகத்தில் இருந்து வடக்கு மற்றும் கிழக்குப் பகுதிகளில் வசிக்கும் இலங்கைத் தமிழர்களுக்கு அனுப்புவதற்கான தமிழக அரசின் உறுதிப்பாட்டை மீண்டும் வலியுறுத்தினார். இலங்கை, அத்துடன் தோட்டங்களில் வேலை செய்பவர்கள்”. முன்னதாக ஏப்ரல் 7 ஆம் தேதி அவருடன் தொலைபேசி உரையாடலில் அவர் இந்த பிரச்சினையை எழுப்பினார்.

அனைத்து இலங்கையர்களுக்கும் உதவும் தீர்மானத்திற்கு முன்னதாக பேரவையில் அறிக்கையொன்றை வெளியிட்ட ஸ்டாலின், இலங்கை எவ்வாறு இவ்வாறானதொரு நிலைமைக்கு வந்தது என்பதை பற்றி பேச விரும்பவில்லை என தெரிவித்தார். அதேவேளை, இலங்கை மக்கள் எதிர்நோக்கும் இன்னல்கள் தமிழகத்தில் உள்ள அனைவரையும் சோகத்தில் ஆழ்த்தியது. உயிர் காக்கும் மருந்துகள் கூட கண்டுபிடிக்க கடினமாக இருந்தது. அனைத்து அத்தியாவசியப் பொருட்களின் விலையும் சாமானியர்களுக்கு எட்டாத அளவுக்கு உயர்ந்துள்ளது. “இது ஒரு அண்டை நாட்டின் பிரச்சினை என்று நினைத்துக்கொண்டு நாம் நம் வாழ்க்கையைத் தொடர முடியாது. யார் ஆட்சியில் இருக்கிறார்கள், அவர்கள் எப்படிப்பட்டவர்கள் என்ற விபரங்களை எடுத்துரைக்க வேண்டிய நேரம் இதுவல்ல,” என ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷ பாதுகாப்புச் செயலாளராகவும், தற்போதைய பிரதமர் மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவைக் குறிப்பிட்டுள்ளார். விடுதலைப் புலிகளின் தலைவர் வி. பிரபாகரன் கொல்லப்பட்டபோதும், ஈழப்போர் முடிவுக்கு வந்தபோது ஆயிரக்கணக்கான தமிழர்கள் கொல்லப்பட்டதும் ஜனாதிபதி.

“நம்மால் முடிந்த உதவியை செய்ய வேண்டும். அங்குள்ள பிரச்சனைகள் பற்றிய செய்திகள் வர ஆரம்பித்தவுடன், ஈழத் தமிழர்களுக்கு எங்களால் முடிந்த அனைத்து வழிகளிலும் உதவுவோம் என்று அறிவித்திருந்தேன். இலங்கையில் உள்ள சில தமிழ் அமைப்புகள், தமிழர்களுக்கு மட்டும் நிவாரணம் அனுப்பாமல், அனைத்து இலங்கையர்களுக்கும் அனுப்பி வைக்குமாறு என்னிடம் கோரிக்கை விடுத்தனர். ‘தயவுசெய்து மக்களை அவர்களின் இன அடையாளங்களால் பார்க்காதீர்கள். நாங்கள் அனைவரும் ஒரே மாதிரியான பிரச்சினைகளை எதிர்கொள்கிறோம்’ என்று அவர்கள் எங்களிடம் தெரிவித்தனர். இதை கேட்டதும் நான் உணர்ச்சிவசப்பட்டேன்.... இதுதான் தமிழ் கலாச்சாரம். ‘நல்ல உள்ளம் பகைவரையும் ஆசிர்வதிக்கும்’ என்ற கொள்கையில் இலங்கைத் தமிழர்கள் வாழ்கிறார்கள் என்பதையே இது காட்டுகிறது.” (கவிஞர் சுப்ரமணிய பாரதி எழுதிய வரி இது.

ஏப்ரல் 29 அன்று, தமிழ்நாடு சட்டமன்றம் ஒரு திருத்தப்பட்ட தீர்மானத்தை நிறைவேற்றியது, அதன் முந்தைய நிலைப்பாட்டை மாற்றி, அனைத்து இலங்கை மக்களுக்கும் மனிதாபிமான உதவிகளை வழங்க தயாராக இருப்பதாகக் கூறியது. அதே நாளில், நரேந்திர மோடிக்கு ஸ்டாலின் எழுதிய கடிதத்தில், “எங்கள் சுற்றுப்புறத்தில் சொல்லொணா துன்பங்கள் மற்றும் அமைதியின்மை பற்றிய செய்திகள் ஒவ்வொரு நாளும் கொட்டுகின்றன. இந்த இக்கட்டான சூழ்நிலையை கருத்தில் கொண்டு, தமிழக சட்டப் பேரவையின் அவையில் இன்று ஏகமனதாக ஒரு தீர்மானம் நிறைவேற்றப்பட்டு, எங்களின் கோரிக்கையை சாதகமாக பரிசீலிக்க வேண்டும் என்று இந்திய அரசை வலியுறுத்துகிறது.

தமிழ்த் தேசியக் கூட்டமைப்பின் தலைவர் எம்.ஏ.சுமந்திரன் அறிக்கையை வரவேற்றதுடன், “எங்கள் கோரிக்கைக்கு செவிசாய்த்து அனைத்து இலங்கையர்களுக்கும் உதவிகளை வழங்கிய தமிழக முதலமைச்சருக்கு மனமார்ந்த நன்றி” என்று தெரிவித்தார். தமிழ் முற்போக்குக் கூட்டணியின் தலைவரும் கொழும்பு எம்.பி.யுமான மனோ கணேசன் ஏப்ரல் 29 அன்று ட்வீட் செய்ததாவது: “எல்லா மக்களுக்கும் கிட்டத்தட்ட 6000 மில்லியன் ரூபாய் மதிப்பில் உணவு மற்றும் மருந்துகளை கப்பல் மூலம் நன்கொடையாக அனுப்பும் மனப்பூர்வமான முடிவிற்கு தமிழக முதல்வர் ஸ்டாலினுக்கு நன்றி. இலங்கை. தமிழக அரசின் இந்த உதவிப் பணிக்கு இந்திய அரசின் ஒப்புதலை இப்போது எதிர்பார்க்கிறோம்.

ஸ்டாலினின் மார்ச் 31 கோரிக்கைக்கு பதிலளிக்க மறுத்த மத்திய அரசு இறுதியாக தமிழக அரசின் கோரிக்கையை ஏற்க ஒப்புக்கொண்டது. மே 1 அன்று, ஜெய்சங்கர் ஸ்டாலினுக்கு எழுதிய கடிதத்தில், "அரசு-அரசாங்கம் என்ற அடிப்படையில் உள்ளடங்கிய உதவிகளை" ஏற்க இலங்கை தயாராக இருப்பதாகக் கூறினார். "எனவே, இந்த விஷயத்தை எங்கள் உயர் ஸ்தானிகராலயத்தால் ஒருங்கிணைக்க முடியும், ஏற்கனவே உள்ள உதவிக்கு கூடுதலாக," என்று அவர் கூறினார், மேலும் மனிதாபிமான நிவாரணங்களை வழங்குவதற்கும் விநியோகிப்பதற்கும் தமிழ்நாடு தலைமைச் செயலாளர் இந்திய அரசாங்கத்துடன் ஒருங்கிணைக்க முடியும் என்றும் கூறினார்.

மே 4 அன்று, அப்போதைய இலங்கைப் பிரதமர் மகிந்த ராஜபக்சே, ஸ்டாலினுக்கு எழுதிய கடிதத்தில், இந்த முயற்சிக்கு நன்றி தெரிவித்து, “நாட்டின் நெருக்கடியை மனிதாபிமானமாகப் பார்த்ததற்காக உங்களுக்கும், தமிழக அரசுக்கும் இலங்கை மக்கள் சார்பாக நன்றியைத் தெரிவித்துக் கொள்கிறேன். வேறொரு நாட்டைப் பற்றிய பிரச்சினையாக இருப்பதை விட நிலைப்பாட்டில்."

இலங்கையில் உள்ள பிரச்சனை தமிழ் பேசும் மக்களை பாதிக்கிறது என்ற உண்மையிலிருந்து தமிழகத்தின் நடவடிக்கைகள் உருவாகும் அதே வேளையில், இந்திய அரசு அகதிகளின் வருகையைக் கட்டுப்படுத்துவது மற்றும் தமிழ்நாட்டைப் பயன்படுத்தி இந்தியாவுக்கும் இலங்கைக்கும் இடையிலான உறவை மேம்படுத்துவது என்ற கோணத்தில் பார்க்கிறது.

2009க்குப் பிறகு, கொழும்புக்கும் சென்னைக்கும் இடையே இருந்த இரு நாட்டு மக்களுக்கும் இடையேயான வர்த்தகம் மற்றும் பிற தொடர்புகள் துண்டிக்கப்பட்டன. போர்க் காலங்களிலும் அதற்கு முந்தைய காலத்திலும், இரு நாடுகளும் இரண்டு படகுச் சேவைகள் மூலம் இணைக்கப்பட்டன, ஒன்று ராமேஸ்வரம் (தெற்கே மிக அதிகமாக) இந்திய நிலப்பரப்பின் புள்ளி) மற்றும் மன்னார் (இலங்கையின் அருகில் உள்ள புள்ளி), மற்றொன்று தூத்துக்குடி மற்றும் கொழும்பு இடையே.

2011ஆம் ஆண்டு தூத்துக்குடி-கொழும்பு சேவை புத்துயிர் பெற்ற போதிலும், போதிய ஆதரவு இல்லாததால் அந்த சேவை திரும்பப் பெறப்பட்டது. மக்களுக்கிடையிலான உறவுகளில் சாத்தியமான அதிகரிப்புடன், இரு நாட்டு தமிழ் பேசும் மக்களிடையே பாரம்பரிய வர்த்தக வழிகள் மற்றும் பாரம்பரிய இணைப்புகளை புதுப்பிக்க புது தில்லி நம்புகிறது.

ஆனால் இவை அனைத்தும் இலங்கையின் பொருளாதார மீட்சியின் வகையைச் சார்ந்தது. எரிபொருள் பற்றாக்குறை மற்றும் விநியோக பிரச்சினைகள் காரணமாக பொருளாதார நடவடிக்கைகள் "கணிசமான அளவில் பாதிக்கப்படும்" என எதிர்பார்க்கப்படுவதாக இலங்கை மத்திய வங்கி மே 19 அன்று கூறியது. அத்தியாவசிய சேவை ஊழியர்கள் மாத்திரமே மே 20 ஆம் திகதி பணிக்கு சமூகமளிக்க வேண்டும் என பிரதமர் ரணில் விக்ரமசிங்க பாராளுமன்றத்தில் அறிவித்துள்ளார். எரிபொருள் நெருக்கடி காரணமாக ஏனைய அனைத்து ஊழியர்களும் விலகி இருக்குமாறு கேட்டுக் கொள்ளப்பட்டுள்ளனர்.

ஜூன் நடுப்பகுதி வரை தேவையான பெற்றோல் மற்றும் டீசல் ஏற்கனவே கொள்வனவு செய்யப்பட்டுள்ளதாகவும் பிரதமர் பாராளுமன்றத்தில் தெரிவித்தார். முன்னோக்கி செல்லும் பாதையில் எவ்வளவு ஏற்ற இறக்கங்கள் இருக்கும் என்று தோன்றுகிறது.

en.title: Tamil Nadu sends on May 19, the first consignment of rice, milk powder and essential medicines Via Ship  to Sri Lanka signalling a shift in  relations between people of the  South Indian State nd the Island
body_lines: When Tan Binh 99, the Panama-registered general cargo vessel left Chennai at 8.28 a.m. on May 19 with 9,000 tons of rice, 200 tons of milk powder, and 24 tons of essential medicines, it marked a first of sorts: A province in a developing country extending its assistance to another country.

Tamil Nadu decided to extend humanitarian assistance to Sri Lanka in the form of rice and other essential commodities, taking into consideration the plight of the people following the economic deterioration of the island nation. This is a marked shift in the stand of political parties in Tamil Nadu, which has in the past demanded a separate Tamil nation in Sri Lanka. It also sets the stage for enhanced interaction between the people of the two regions.

Chief Minister M.K. Stalin flagged off the process of loading the cargo onto the vessel on May 18. At a function held at West Quay in Chennai Port on May 18, Stalin symbolically handed over a sample pouch of the material to D. Venkateshwaran, Sri Lankan Deputy High Commissioner in Southern India.. The cargo vessel is expected to cover the distance of just over 400 nautical miles in about two days.

The total aid promised by the Tamil Nadu government is worth Rs.123 crore and consists of 40,000 tons of rice, 100-plus life-saving drugs and 500 tons of milk powder. This is just the first consignment and is being sent with the consent of the Government of India. The State government has appealed to the people to contribute generously to the relief efforts in Sri Lanka.

In a Legislative Assembly resolution, all the political parties represented in the Tamil Nadu State Assembly had expressed their anxiety over the rising prices of essential commodities in Sri Lanka and the implications for India if the situation did not improve.

Tamil Nadu had extensive trade, cultural, and cricketing ties with Sri Lanka, but the relationship came to a standstill after the crushing defeat of the the Liberation Tamils of Tamil Eelam (LTTE) in May 2009. In fact, Tamil nationalist groups and fringe elements in the State had agitated against the training given to Sri Lankan officers in defence facilities located in the State and had protested the presence of Sri Lankan cricketers in the Chennai franchise of the Indian Premier League. On its part, the Sri Lankan government stipulated that no Sri Lankan government official could enter Tamil Nadu or transit via the Chennai airport given the security situation.

Tamil Nadu took the first step on March 31, when Chief Minister M.K. Stalin raised the issue of helping Sri Lankan Tamils with Prime Minister Narendra Modi as a trickle of refugees began arriving in the State from Sri Lanka when the economic situation in Sri Lanka worsened. The first set of refugees—technically economic refugees since there was no war in Sri Lanka—arrived in the State on March 8. By April 25, the number of refugees swelled to 75. “Many more would have crossed over but for the Sri Lankan Navy arresting or sending back Sri Lankan Tamils,” a senior Tamil Nadu officer told Frontline.

Tamil Nadu’s offer was welcomed by the Tamil political parties in Sri Lanka. The Tamil National Alliance (TNA), the largest umbrella alliance of Tamil political parties in Sri Lanka, welcomed the move. But, after a few days, given the new-found bonhomie between all sections of the people in Sri Lanka, the TNA requested the Tamil Nadu government to include all Sri Lankans in the proposed material aid package.

There was a lull soon after, with the Indian government not taking a decision on Tamil Nadu’s request. On April 15, Stalin wrote to the External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar, reiterating the Tamil Nadu government’s commitment to “ship essential supplies including food grain, vegetables, and medicines from Thoothukudi Port to the Sri Lankan Tamils living in the northern and eastern parts of Sri Lanka, as well as those working in the plantations”. He had earlier raised the issue in a telephone conversation with him on April 7.

Making a statement in the Assembly ahead of a resolution helping all Sri Lankans, Stalin said that he did not want to dwell into how Sri Lanka ended up in a situation of this nature. At the same time, the hardship faced by the people of Sri Lanka had saddened everyone in Tamil Nadu. Even life-saving medicines were hard to find. The price of all essential items had gone up beyond reach of the common man. “We cannot merely carry on with our lives thinking that this is the problem of a neighbouring country. This is not the time to be drawn into details of who is in power and what kind of people they are,” he said in a reference to the fact that President Gotabaya Rajapaksa was the Defence Secretary and the current Prime Minister, Mahinda Rajapaksa was the President when LTTE chief V. Prabhakaran was killed and thousands of Tamils were murdered as the Eelam war came to a close.

“We have to help in whatever way we can. As soon as we began getting news of the problems there, I had announced that we will help Eelam Tamils in all the ways that we can…. Some Tamil organisations in Sri Lanka requested me not to send relief only for Tamils, but to send it to all Sri Lankans. ‘Please do not view people by their ethnic identities. All of us are facing the same problems,’ they informed us. When I heard this, I become emotional…. This is Tamil culture. This goes to show that Sri Lankan Tamils are living to the maxim ‘the good heart will bless even the enemy.’” (This is a line penned by poet Subramaniya Bharati—pagaivanukkum arulvai nannenje.)

On April 29, the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly passed a revised resolution, altering its earlier stance and saying that it was willing to provide humanitarian assistance to all Sri Lankan people. The same day, in a letter addressed to Narendra Modi, Stalin said: “Reports of untold sufferings and unrest in our neighbourhood has been pouring in every day. Considering this critical situation, the august House of the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly has unanimously passed a resolution today, urging the Government of India to positively consider our request to immediately despatch the much needed supplies.”

TNA leader M.A. Sumanthiran welcomed the statement and conveyed his “heartfelt thanks to the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister for heeding to our request and providing assistance to all Sri Lankans”. Mano Ganesan, Tamil Progressive Alliance leader and Colombo MP, tweeted on April 29: “Thank you CM Stalin of TN for the bighearted decision to send a shipload donation of food and medicine to the value of nearly LKR 6000 million to all the people of Sri Lanka. We now foresee the consents of the Government of India to this aid mission of Government of Tamil Nadu.”

After refusing to respond Stalin’s March 31 demand, the Union government finally agreed to allow the request of the Tamil Nadu government. On May 1, Jaishankar wrote to Stalin saying that Sri Lanka was willing to accept “inclusive aid on a Government-to-Government basis”. “The matter could, therefore, be coordinated by our High Commission, supplementing assistance already underway,” he said and added that the Tamil Nadu Chief Secretary could coordinate with Government of India for the supply and distribution of humanitarian relief.

On May 4, the then Sri Lankan Prime Minister Mahinda Rajapaksa wrote to Stalin, thanking him for the initiative: “I wish to thank you and the Tamil Nadu government on behalf of the people of Sri Lanka, for viewing the country’s crisis from a humanitarian standpoint, rather than as a problem concerning another country.”

While Tamil Nadu’s actions stem from the fact that the problem in Sri Lanka affects the Tamil-speaking people, the Government of India views it from the angle of controlling refugee inflows and improving relations between India and Sri Lanka using Tamil Nadu.

The trade and other connections between the people of the two countries, predominantly between Colombo and Chennai, had wound down after 2009. During the war years and earlier, the two countries were connected by two ferry services, one between Rameswaram (the southern-most point of Indian mainland) and Mannar (the nearest point in Sri Lanka), and the other between Thoothukudi and Colombo.

Though the Thoothukudi-Colombo service was revived in 2011, the service had to be withdrawn for lack of patronage. With the possible increase in people-to-people ties, New Delhi hopes to revive the traditional trade routes as well as the traditional links between the Tamil-speaking people of both countries.

But all these hinge on the kind of economic recovery in Sri Lanka. The Sri Lankan Central Bank said on May 19 that economic activity is expected to be “affected considerably” because of the fuel shortage and supply issues. Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe announced in Parliament that only essential service staff had to report for work on May 20. All other employees have been asked to stay away because of the fuel crisis.
The Prime Minister also informed Parliament that petrol and diesel required up to mid-June has already been procured. It appears that there will be as many ups as downs in the road ahead.
